paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/909/Hu et al. - 2025 - MoEQuant Enhancing Quantization for Mixture-of-Experts Large Language Models via Expert-Balanced Sa.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a systems/ML quantization paper without letting its strong calibration vocabulary dominate the cross-domain schema.
expected_result: paper.md should explain the MoE-specific PTQ mechanism, distinguish weight-only from weight-activation settings when applicable, and route evidence through expert calibration, model quality, and systems measurements.
acceptable_paths: A reusable PTQ/MoE method family with paper-specific components in aliases or mechanism contracts.
Source-quality hold if extraction is genuinely insufficient, with no scientific claims promoted.
must_not_do: Do not make paper-specific component names the only methods/topics.
Do not collapse expert-routing evidence into generic quantization accuracy.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism contracts explain inputs, transforms, outputs, assumptions, and first implementation checks.
Frontmatter separates methods, topics, settings, aliases, datasets, and metrics.
When-to-retrieve examples are standalone research intents, not title-aware prompts.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/936/Shah et al. - 2024 - FlashAttention-3 Fast and Accurate Attention with Asynchrony and Low-precision.pdf
problem_description: Ingest an attention/kernel architecture paper where the core mechanism is scheduling, IO, precision, and hardware overlap, not quantization as a research object.
expected_result: paper.md should teach the attention-kernel mechanism, GPU/hardware setting, IO or asynchrony constraints, accuracy/runtime evidence, and implementation hooks for kernel correctness and profiling.
acceptable_paths: Classify as attention acceleration / hardware-aware attention kernel, with low-precision as a setting if needed.
Source-quality hold if extraction fails.
must_not_do: Do not route it as generic LLM PTQ just because low precision appears.
Do not replace kernel scheduling/asynchrony details with generic attention prose.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism captures what changes in attention computation and what assumptions make it faster.
Evidence map separates runtime, numerical accuracy, and hardware-specific claims.
Implementation hooks mention profiling, kernel correctness, memory/IO, or precision checks.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/992/Rafailov et al. - 2024 - Direct Preference Optimization Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a preference-optimization paper where the core mechanism is the objective transformation from reward modeling/RLHF into direct policy optimization.
expected_result: paper.md should explain the preference-pair objective, reference-policy/KL assumptions, what DPO removes or replaces from RLHF, and evidence around preference/alignment evaluation.
acceptable_paths: Use preference optimization / reward modeling / RLHF methods and preference-learning setting.
If extraction is weak, preserve uncertainty instead of inventing objective details.
must_not_do: Do not classify this primarily as quantization, generic policy optimization, or benchmark-only work.
Do not summarize it as only 'uses human feedback to improve models'.
evaluation_rubric: A new researcher can explain how the optimization objective works at a high level.
Evidence and limitations separate paper claims from wiki synthesis.
Implementation hooks mention preference data construction, reference model/KL, loss checks, baselines, and eval protocol.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/1899/Zuo et al. - 2025 - TTRL Test-Time Reinforcement Learning.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a test-time reinforcement learning paper where the core value is optimizing model behavior during inference or problem solving rather than offline preference tuning.
expected_result: paper.md should route to test-time RL / policy optimization, explain the update loop and reward/evaluation assumptions, and separate evidence for improved reasoning from risks such as reward specification or overfitting to test-time signals.
acceptable_paths: Use test-time reinforcement learning, policy optimization, or RL reasoning methods depending on extracted content.
Source-quality hold if the PDF is not readable.
must_not_do: Do not collapse it into RLHF/preference optimization unless the paper explicitly uses preference labels.
Do not treat it as generic chain-of-thought prompting.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism states what is updated at test time and what reward or feedback signal drives it.
Evidence map distinguishes reasoning/eval gains, baseline fairness, and scope limits.
Implementation hooks mention rollout/update logging, reward checks, seed variance, and control baselines.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/2013/Cai et al. - 2025 - PyramidKV Dynamic KV Cache Compression based on Pyramidal Information Funneling.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a long-context/KV-cache memory paper where the core mechanism is dynamic cache retention or compression during decoding.
expected_result: paper.md should explain the KV retention/compression rule, when memory is saved, how quality is evaluated at long context, and which implementation checks prevent cache-policy bugs.
acceptable_paths: Classify as KV-cache compression / long-context inference with memory or latency evidence.
Source-quality hold if extraction fails.
must_not_do: Do not classify it as quantization just because cache or compression appears.
Do not omit sequence-length, retention-ratio, and quality/runtime tradeoffs.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism contract covers KV-cache tensors, retention budget, policy/score, and output behavior.
Evidence map separates long-context quality, memory, latency, and retention ratio.
Implementation hooks mention cache tensor shapes, sequence-length sweeps, and policy ablations.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/2086/Ye et al. - 2025 - Speculative Actions A Lossless Framework for Faster Agentic Systems.pdf
problem_description: Ingest an agent workflow paper where the value is action-level speculative execution and correctness/latency tradeoffs in agentic systems.
expected_result: paper.md should explain agent state/action execution, speculation/verification or rollback boundaries, losslessness claims, and evaluation evidence around task success and speed.
acceptable_paths: Route as agent workflow acceleration, speculative execution, or tool-use/action verification depending on extracted text.
Source-quality hold if the PDF is not readable.
must_not_do: Do not collapse it into generic speculative decoding unless the paper itself is token decoding.
Do not write generic agent benchmark boilerplate without explaining the workflow mechanism.
evaluation_rubric: Methods/topics/settings distinguish agent workflow from LLM decoding.
When-to-retrieve examples are plausible research intents around agent systems.
Implementation hooks mention traces, action correctness, rollback/verification, tool/environment state, or latency measurement.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/1837/Chu et al. - 2023 - Qwen-Audio Advancing Universal Audio Understanding via Unified Large-Scale Audio-Language Models.pdf
problem_description: Ingest an audio-language model paper where the mechanism spans audio encoder, language model interface, task formatting, and multimodal evaluation.
expected_result: paper.md should route to audio-language modeling, multimodal instruction/evaluation, and implementation hooks for audio preprocessing, encoder/LLM alignment, and task-specific error analysis.
acceptable_paths: Use audio-language modeling or multimodal model methods/settings with datasets/metrics if detected.
Source-quality hold if extraction fails.
must_not_do: Do not misclassify audio-language as vision-language or quantization.
Do not treat the technical report as only a dataset list.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism explains the audio-to-language interface and training/eval setup.
Evidence map separates task families and metrics.
Retrieval examples support complex multimodal research use, not title lookup.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/1333/Assran et al. - 2025 - V-JEPA 2 Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a visual representation/world-modeling paper where mechanism is latent prediction and downstream planning/understanding, not text-generation retrieval.
expected_result: paper.md should explain representation/prediction mechanism, video or image latent setting, evidence across perception/planning tasks, and implementation hooks for representation probes and ablations.
acceptable_paths: Classify as joint embedding predictive learning, video representation learning, or world-model learning.
Use source-quality hold if extraction is insufficient.
must_not_do: Do not force it into quantization, RLHF, or generic transformer architecture.
Do not reduce it to 'self-supervised learning improves performance'.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism teaches what is predicted, what representation is learned, and how evidence supports it.
Topics/methods/settings are reusable across representation-learning papers.
Implementation hooks include representation probes, task split, and ablation sanity checks.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/1361/Raissi et al. - 2019 - Physics-informed neural networks A deep learning framework for solving forward and inverse problems.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a scientific ML/PINN paper where the method is PDE residual-constrained learning for forward/inverse problems.
expected_result: paper.md should explain PDE residuals, boundary/initial conditions, collocation/data losses, forward versus inverse evaluation, and implementation hooks for autodiff residual checks.
acceptable_paths: Classify as physics-informed neural networks / PDE-constrained learning.
If extraction is weak, mark uncertainty without hallucinating equations.
must_not_do: Do not classify 'support' text as PPO or RL.
Do not make it an ML benchmark paper without PDE constraints.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism teaches the residual/data loss structure and assumptions.
Evidence map identifies forward/inverse problem evaluation, metrics, or case studies.
Implementation hooks include toy PDE residual unit tests and loss-term logging.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/1250/Ding and He - 2004 - K -means clustering via principal component analysis.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a classical clustering theory paper where the value is the relation between K-means objective and PCA, not non-uniform quantization.
expected_result: paper.md should route to clustering theory/PCA relationship, state the objective or theorem-level mechanism, and provide implementation/sanity checks around centroids, objective, and assumptions.
acceptable_paths: Use clustering algorithm / clustering theory setting and PCA relationship topics.
Source-quality hold for bad scan text.
must_not_do: Do not inherit quantization routing because K-means and centroids appear.
Do not make topics generic like error, design, methods, or performance.
evaluation_rubric: Methods/topics/settings have no quantization pollution.
Mechanism explains clustering/PCA relation enough for a new researcher.
Evidence/uncertainty separates theorem assumptions from empirical use.

paper_path: /Users/shawn/Desktop/我的文库/files/2264/Wang 等 - 2024 - A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents.pdf
problem_description: Ingest a survey where the wiki value is taxonomy, gaps, and routing to primary work, not a single method implementation.
expected_result: paper.md should identify it as survey synthesis, capture taxonomy/useful dimensions, uncertainty about primary evidence, and retrieval value for agent research planning.
acceptable_paths: Classify as survey synthesis / LLM agents / agent planning.
Use source-quality hold if extraction is not usable.
must_not_do: Do not invent a single proposed method.
Do not treat survey claims as primary experimental evidence.
evaluation_rubric: Mechanism section adapts to survey shape: taxonomy and primary-paper followups rather than fake algorithm contracts.
Limitations warn that claims need primary-source validation.
When-to-retrieve examples support literature mapping and gap finding.

problem_description: Ingest a bad-metadata or bad-source PDF and verify the workflow produces a source-quality hold instead of hallucinated paper knowledge.
expected_result: paper.md should be a source-quality hold with managed source path, extraction reasons, OCR/replacement next steps, and no scientific claims promoted.
acceptable_paths: Source-quality hold with source_text_insufficient or equivalent review state.
If the PDF is actually readable, it must still distrust bad metadata and use extracted title/content.
must_not_do: Do not make a normal paper summary from a cover page or metadata-only file.
Do not promote claims, topics, or methods beyond source-management state if text is insufficient.
evaluation_rubric: Source facts and source-quality state are separated from paper knowledge.
Retrieval examples route only to OCR/source cleanup.
Candidate records are safe to promote as source-management state, not scientific content.
